title: acciddasuite
text: acciddasuite builds infectious disease forecasts steps: get_data() check_data(): fetch validate surveillance data. get_ncast() (optional): correct recent weeks reporting delays. get_cv() (optional): evaluate candidate models time series cross-validation. get_fcast(): ensemble best models forward-looking forecast. package relies fable modeling framework follows standard forecasting workflow described Hyndman & Athanasopoulos (2021). overall goal provide public health professionals easily-adoptable approach generating, evaluating forecasts, visualizing infectious disease forecasts. get information know whether forecasting best approach task, follow steps article.

title: acciddasuite
text: fetch weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions New York CDC NHSN via epidatr. Setting revisions = TRUE retrieves full revision history (.e. past versions data), needed nowcasting. get_data() returns validated accidda_data object: can also bring data. Just pass check_data(). See vignette("external_data") formatting details.
code: library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ny", revisions = TRUE) df #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: NY  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-06-06 ( 305 dates ) #> Interval: 7 days #> History:  TRUE ( 2024-11-17 to 2026-06-07 )

title: acciddasuite
text: recent weeks surveillance data almost always low hospitals still filing late reports (right truncated). feed raw counts forecaster, predictions biased downward. get_ncast() estimates recent counts look like reports arrive. default max_delay = 4, last 4 weeks corrected; everything left untouched.  corrected ncast$data contains two extra columns: ncast_lower ncast_upper (95% CrI) corrected weeks. get_fcast() detects automatically uses propagate nowcasting uncertainty final forecast.
code: ncast <- get_ncast(df) ncast #> <accidda_ncast> #>  #> Location: NY  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Nowcasted 2 weeks: 2026-05-30 to 2026-06-06  #>  #> $data  corrected series (305 rows) #> $plot  nowcast visualisation ncast$plot

title: acciddasuite
text: Forecasting split two steps: get_cv() — model selection: time series cross-validation full (median corrected) series, starting eval_start_date. Models ranked WIS interval coverage. get_fcast() — final forecast: reuses ranking ensemble best top_n models projects h weeks future. nowcast columns present, forecast produced three baselines (lower, median, upper nowcast estimates) pooled, prediction intervals reflect model uncertainty nowcast uncertainty. set eval_start_date mark start evaluation window. least 52 weeks data must precede date. Default models : NAIVE (Naïve / random walk): Carries last observed value forward. simplest possible baseline. ETS (Exponential Smoothing): weighted average recent weeks matter older ones. Adapts trends seasonal patterns. THETA: Splits data long-term trend short-term fluctuations, forecasts separately, combines . ARIMA: Learns repeating patterns past values predict future ones. Auto-configured find best fit.
code: eval_start_date <- max(ncast$data$target_end_date) - 28 cv <- get_cv(   ncast,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   h = 4 ) cv #> <accidda_cv> #>  #> Models ranked (cross-validation): #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   model_id   wis #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 ETS       29.4 #> 2 THETA     34.1 #> 3 NAIVE     35.1 #>  #> Evaluated from 2026-05-09 | horizon 4 weeks | NY  #>  #> Contents: #>   $forecasts  per-origin forecasts (model_out_tbl) #>   $oracle     observed truth (oracle_output) #>   $score      model ranking table #>   $models     model specifications #>   $meta       eval_start_date, h, location, target, interval fcast <- get_fcast(cv, top_n = 3) fcast #> <accidda_fcast> #>  #> Models ranked (cross-validation): #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   model_id   wis #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 ETS       29.4 #> 2 THETA     34.1 #> 3 NAIVE     35.1 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-06-13  #>   To:   2026-07-04  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hub    hub forecast object (model_out_tbl, oracle_output) #>   $score  model ranking table, or NULL #>   $meta   models, top_n, h, location, target, interval, nowcast

title: acciddasuite
text: model compatible fable framework can passed get_cv() via models. Compose default_models() keep built-ins alongside :
code: library(fable) library(fable.prophet) library(EpiEstim) library(projections) my_models <- c(   default_models(),   list(     CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1,1,0)),     PROPHET = prophet(observation ~ season("year")),     EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3, std_si = 2, rt_window = 7)   ) )  cv <- get_cv(   ncast,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   h = 3,   models = my_models )  fcast <- get_fcast(cv, top_n = 3)

title: acciddasuite
text: RespiLens platform sharing respiratory disease forecasts. Use to_respilens() export forecast JSON upload MyRespiLens.
code: to_respilens(fcast, "respilens.json")

title: Preparing External Data
text: can use surveillance dataset acciddasuite — just pass check_data() validate enter pipeline.

title: Preparing External Data
text: data frame must 4 columns: enable nowcasting (correcting reporting delays), add 5th column: as_of, target_end_date can appear multiple times (one row per revision). Without , target_end_date appear .

title: Preparing External Data
code: library(acciddasuite) head(df) ##   target_end_date observation location             target ## 1      2024-01-01          13       NY inc hosp influenza ## 2      2024-01-08          15       NY inc hosp influenza ## 3      2024-01-15          19       NY inc hosp influenza ## 4      2024-01-22          22       NY inc hosp influenza ## 5      2024-01-29          25       NY inc hosp influenza ## 6      2024-02-05          11       NY inc hosp influenza checked <- check_data(df) checked ## <accidda_data> ##  ## Location: NY  ## Target:   inc hosp influenza  ## Window:   2024-01-01 to 2024-12-23 ( 52 dates ) ## Interval: 7 days ## History:  FALSE

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Disease forecasting invaluable tool field public health, however, clearly defining parameters problems want solve often difficult forecasting . acciddasuite pakcage provides comprehensive toolkit generating forecasts expectation users already concrete idea like forecast. vignette, provide series questions aid forecasters discovery “”, “”, “” “” question. package documentation still actively development, welcome contributions feedback community.

what: Step 1: Why are we interested in forecasting?
title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: First, needs clearly defined project get started. set questions consider begin process: question attempting answer? insights hope gain? Determine trying gain forecasting project. Specificity stage makes future stages easier! audience, , benefit insights? Determine use forecasts, interpretation benefit. far future interested forecasting? far future insights need useful? *full seasonsal projections known “scenarios” different forecasts. time frame associated question determines whether forecasting best tool. Read Step 2… STOP! defined forecasting problem using questions ? YES → Proceed next step → Continue defining approach

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Determine central question strictly forecast, aligned scenario projection. Forecasts concerned happen future current conditions/regardless interventions take place. , can determine actionables deploy resources, starting point based “real life” assumption (.e., unconditional projection). e.g., want advanced notice influenza hospitalization burden coming weeks. Scenarios concerned happen take action X? , future differe X happens instead Y? Often, scenarios, comparing different outcomes basis different assumptions (.e., conditional projection). e.g., want know predicted burden 50% population vaccinated flu vs. 30%. want produce projections future weeks time, question likely better suited scenario projection rather forecast (forecasts give us look 1-4 weeks ahead given start date, scenarios show us entire seasons time). information scenario projections, visit Scenario Modeling Hub. STOP! confirmed question best answered forecast? YES → Proceed next step → Consider checking scenario projections instead!

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Next, define pathogen, target (data stream), spatial unit involved forecasting project. pathogen interested forecasting? e.g., influenza, COVID-19, RSV, etc. data stream interested forecasting (also known target)? Examples common forecasting targets “incidence hospitalization” (patients certain hospitalization), “percent emergency department visits” (attributable certain pathogen), “deaths” (due certain pathogen), “hospital bed occupancy” (patients certain pathogen). targets measures disease burden give public health professionals idea much disease population bears given time. common forecasting target respiratory illnesses “incidence hospitalization”, makes easiest find data . Presently, acciddasuite forecasts targets “incidence hospitalization” “death”. spatial unit provide best insight? data available scale? e.g., national, state, county, city, health jurisdiction, hospital system, even facility (e.g., hospital). granular spatial unit, difficult find data, often trade-data specificity availability. Note can forecast multiple locations (e.g., multiple states health jurisdictions) , want forecast multiple pathogens targets, best separate distinct forecasts. STOP! defined pathogen, target, spatial unit? YES → Proceed next step → Continue defining data elements

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: context, forecasts mathematical predictions weeks ahead given starting point “ground truth” data (.e, information already happened). , need provide forecasting models ground truth data matches resolution forecasting question (.e., pathogen, target, locations). Data availability often limiting factor considering forecasting question. step, provide decision tree approach determine can forecast ground truth data already exists, need provide specialized data complete forecast, already forecasting infrastructure answers forecasting question. easy way find either ground truth data forecasts via forecast hub. Forecasting hubs (organized hubverse) standardized repositories disease forecasts ground truth data data follows structured guidelines. Ground truth data found forecasting hubs forecast-ready, fact, forecasts may answer forecasting question(s) without action . example, forecasting RSV, COVID-19, influenza U.S. national state level*, forecasting question likely already answered forecasting hub: RSV: GitHub repository | RespiLens visualization COVID-19: GitHub repository | RespiLens visualization *also hub sub-state level influenza forecasts (GitHub repository | RespiLens visualization) complete list hubverse forecast hubs, see .

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: decide create forecasts find suitable ground truth data, must confirm ground truth data stream stable enough support repeatable workflow. pulling ground truth data hubverse hub, data likely released weekly cadence mostly complete. can find data target-data/ directory hub’s GitHub repository. Alternatively, acciddasuite built-function (get_data()) handle collection + formatting state level respiratory data. want use another ground truth data source, first validate check_data(). Please see external data article information external data source formatting. reporting delays data stream, inconsistencies often fixed later wait , use get_ncast() function correct recent weeks reporting delays.

what: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: acciddasuite provides simple pipeline infectious diseases forecasts. validates input data (check_data()), optionally applies nowcasting adjust reporting delays (get_ncast()), evaluates models cross-validation (get_cv()), generates forecasts (get_fcast()).

title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: can install development version acciddasuite GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") #pak::pak("ACCIDDA/acciddasuite")

title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: Save myRespiLens format:
code: library(acciddasuite) head(example_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>   <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #> 1 2024-11-17 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 2 2024-11-24 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 3 2024-12-01 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 4 2024-12-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 5 2024-12-15 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 6 2024-12-22 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 fcast <- example_data |>   check_data() |>   get_ncast() |>   get_cv(eval_start_date = max(example_data$target_end_date) - 28) |>   get_fcast(top_n = 3) #> ℹ Using max_delay = 6 from data #> ℹ Truncating from max_delay = 6 to 2. #> [2026-06-17 19:07:08.646] get_cv: +0.7772 secs #> [2026-06-17 19:07:09.427] get_fcast: +2.2583 secs  fcast #> <accidda_fcast> #>  #> Models ranked (cross-validation): #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   model_id   wis #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 THETA     26.3 #> 2 ARIMA     28.6 #> 3 ETS       30.4 #> 4 SNAIVE   256.  #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-03-21  #>   To:   2026-04-11  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hub    hub forecast object (model_out_tbl, oracle_output) #>   $score  model ranking table, or NULL #>   $meta   models, top_n, h, location, target, interval, nowcast to_respilens(fcast, path = "respilens.json")

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: fable model estimates reproduction number Rt EpiEstim::estimate_R() projects future incidence projections::project(), use inside model. aggregation period read tsibble index; Rt estimated recent data used simulate n_sim forward paths, returned sample distributions.

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
code: EPIESTIM(   formula,   mean_si,   std_si,   rt_window = 14L,   n_sim = 100L,   R_fix_within = TRUE )

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: formula Response variable, e.g. observation. Exogenous regressors supported. mean_si, std_si Mean SD serial interval, days. rt_window Sliding window width (days) Rt estimation. Smaller tracks recent trends; larger smoother. Default 14. n_sim Number simulated forecast paths. Default 100. R_fix_within TRUE, hold Rt constant within simulated path (recommended short horizons).

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: model definition use inside model.

title: Validate surveillance data — check_data
text: Validates surveillance data frame returns typed accidda_data object rest pipeline accepts without re-validating.

title: Validate surveillance data — check_data
code: check_data(data)

title: Validate surveillance data — check_data
text: data data frame target_end_date (Date), observation (numeric), location (character) target (character). optional as_of (Date) column enables nowcasting (get_ncast). existing accidda_data returned unchanged.

title: Validate surveillance data — check_data
text: accidda_data object: data Validated data frame coerced types. location, target Single location / target identifier. window Named / dates. interval Reporting interval days (7 = weekly). history TRUE revision history (as_of) present.

title: Validate surveillance data — check_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x <- get_data("covid", "ny") |> check_data() my_x <- read.csv("my_data.csv") |> check_data() } # }

title: Default forecasting models — default_models
text: built-model set get_cv get_fcast: naive random walk, ETS, Theta ARIMA, fitted log(observation) stabilise count variance (fable back-transforms automatically). Extend rather replace composing, e.g. c(default_models(), list(CUSTOM = fable::ARIMA(observation))).

title: Default forecasting models — default_models
code: default_models()

title: Default forecasting models — default_models
text: named list fable model definitions.

title: Default forecasting models — default_models
code: default_models() #> $NAIVE #> <RW model definition> #>  #> $ETS #> <ETS model definition> #>  #> $THETA #> <theta model definition> #>  #> $ARIMA #> <ARIMA model definition> #>

title: Weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions for New York — example_data
code: example_data

title: Weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions for New York — example_data
text: data frame 5 columns: as_of Date observation reported. location State abbreviation ("NY"). target Forecast target ("wk inc covid hosp"). target_end_date End date epiweek. observation Confirmed hospital admissions count.

title: Weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions for New York — example_data
text: CDC NHSN via pub_covidcast.

title: Weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions for New York — example_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ example_data |> check_data() } # }

title: Cross-validate forecasting models — get_cv
text: Evaluates fable models expanding-window time-series cross-validation: eval_start_date, models refit origin forecast h steps ahead, scored weighted interval score (WIS) interval coverage via hubEvals. ranking reused get_fcast.

title: Cross-validate forecasting models — get_cv
code: get_cv(x, eval_start_date, h = 4, models = default_models(), step = h)

title: Cross-validate forecasting models — get_cv
text: x accidda_ncast (get_ncast) accidda_data (check_data / get_data). eval_start_date Date (string coercible one). First origin evaluated; must lie within data window. observations form initial training window. h Integer. Forecast horizon, reporting-interval steps (weeks weekly data, days daily). Default 4. models Named list fable models. Defaults default_models; compose c(default_models(),   list(...)) extend. element must reference observation. See fabletools extension vignette custom models. step Integer. Number reporting-interval steps training window advances successive CV origins. Defaults h (non-overlapping evaluation blocks).

title: Cross-validate forecasting models — get_cv
text: accidda_cv object: forecasts Per-origin, per-model forecasts (model_out_tbl). oracle Observed truth (oracle_output). score Model ranking WIS interval coverage. models evaluated model specifications. meta eval_start_date, h, location, target, interval. data Revision-collapsed input, reused get_fcast.

title: Cross-validate forecasting models — get_cv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cv <- get_data("covid", "ny", revisions = TRUE) |>   get_ncast() |>   get_cv(eval_start_date = "2025-01-01", h = 4)  cv$score } # }

title: Fetch hospitalisation data — get_data
text: Fetch confirmed US hospital admissions (COVID-19, influenza RSV) NHSN via pub_covidcast.

title: Fetch hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen, geo_value, revisions = FALSE)

title: Fetch hospitalisation data — get_data
text: pathogen One "covid", "flu" "rsv". geo_value Geographic value(s) fetch, per pub_covidcast. revisions Logical. TRUE, fetch full revision history needed get_ncast. Default FALSE (latest ).

title: Fetch hospitalisation data — get_data
text: accidda_data object (see check_data).

title: Fetch hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ny") #> Warning: No API key found. You will be limited to non-complex queries and encounter rate #> limits if you proceed. #> ℹ See `?save_api_key()` for details on obtaining and setting API keys. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: NY  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-06-06 ( 305 dates ) #> Interval: 7 days #> History:  FALSE  # Revision history for nowcasting get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ca", revisions = TRUE) #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: CA  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-06-06 ( 305 dates ) #> Interval: 7 days #> History:  TRUE ( 2024-11-17 to 2026-06-07 )

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
text: Refits models full series, forecasts h steps ahead combines equal-weight ensemble. Accepts either accidda_cv get_cv — whose ranking selects best top_n models — accidda_data / accidda_ncast, forecasts every model models.

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
code: get_fcast(x, models = default_models(), h = 4, top_n = 3)

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
text: x accidda_cv (get_cv), accidda_ncast accidda_data. models Named list fable models. Defaults default_models. accidda_cv, leave unset forecast top_n ranked models, pass models forecast set . h Integer. Forecast horizon, reporting-interval steps (weeks weekly data). Default 4; accidda_cv, defaults cross-validation horizon. top_n Integer. Number top-ranked CV models ensemble. Used x accidda_cv models unset. Default 3.

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
text: accidda_fcast object: hub Hub-format forecast (model_out_tbl, oracle_output). score Model ranking accidda_cv, NULL. meta models, top_n, h, location, target, interval, nowcast. Export to_respilens.

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
text: input carries ncast_lower / ncast_upper (get_ncast), forecasts pooled across nowcast median 95\

title: Produce a forward-looking forecast — get_fcast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ncast <- get_data("covid", "ny", revisions = TRUE) |> get_ncast() cv    <- ncast |> get_cv(eval_start_date = "2025-01-01", h = 4)  get_fcast(cv, top_n = 3)                 # reuse the cross-validation ranking get_fcast(cv, models = default_models()) # forecast a different set; keeps $score get_fcast(ncast)                         # or forecast the default models directly } # }

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: Recent weeks surveillance data incomplete reporting delays (right truncation). get_ncast estimates final counts baselinenowcast, replacing last max_delay weeks leaving earlier weeks untouched.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
code: get_ncast(x, max_delay = 2, draws = 1000, prop_delay = 0.5, scale_factor = 3)

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: x accidda_data (get_data / check_data) revision history; use get_data(revisions = TRUE). max_delay Integer. Number recent weeks treated right-truncated. Default 2. draws Integer. Number posterior samples. Default 1000. prop_delay Numeric (0, 1). Proportion reference times used delay estimation. Default 0.5. scale_factor Numeric. Multiplier max_delay estimation window. Default 3.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: accidda_ncast object shared backbone (location, target, window, interval, history) plus: data Corrected series. observation holds nowcast median corrected weeks; ncast_lower / ncast_upper (95\ propagate nowcast uncertainty. plot ggplot correction.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: Weekly data ; cadences rejected (rest pipeline cadence-agnostic).

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x     <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ca", revisions = TRUE) ncast <- get_ncast(x) } # }

title: Convert a forecast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
text: Convert forecast RespiLens format

title: Convert a forecast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
code: to_respilens(x, path = NULL)

title: Convert a forecast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
text: x accidda_fcast get_fcast. path Optional .json path write ; NULL, nothing written.

title: Convert a forecast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
text: named list (metadata, ground_truth, forecasts), invisibly.
